root_cause_hypothesis: get_knowledge(topic="reflection-dict") ships a non-compiling example: both the rule line ("DictClass: ... hasStaticMethod(), makeObject()") and the "Runtime enum label + guarded dynamic call" example call hasStaticMethod() on the KERNEL class DictClass. xppc rejects it: "ClassDoesNotContainMethod: Class 'DictClass' does not contain a definition for method 'hasStaticMethod'". hasStaticMethod()/hasObjectMethod() live on the APPLICATION-layer wrapper SysDictClass (ApplicationPlatform, signature: boolean hasStaticMethod(IdentifierName methodName)), which derives from DictClass, so `new SysDictClass(_classId)` + inherited callStatic() is the compiling shape. The case instruction inherits the same error ("constructs a DictClass ... after hasStaticMethod() returned true"). Secondary: validate_code(mode="references") passed the bad call silently — kernel Dict* types have no AOT XML and are absent from the symbol index, so member/arity checking degrades to a no-op there (the mirror image of the known kernel-type unknown-type false positive).
suggested_fix_area: knowledge base: src knowledge topic 'reflection-dict' — correct the DictClass rule line and the guarded-dynamic-call example to SysDictClass (and note DictClass has no has*Method); plus eval/cases/L2-reflection-dict-fieldwalk.json instruction wording.
